title: Compiler may optimize away memset() of hash buffer in lichen_pubkey_to_iid()
description:
In ipv6_addr.c:87, memset(hash, 0, sizeof(hash)) is used to clear the SHA-256 intermediate hash buffer. This memset may be optimized away by the compiler because 'hash' is a local variable that is never read after the memset. The C standard allows dead-store elimination.

Impact: The 32-byte SHA-256 hash of the public key remains in stack memory after lichen_pubkey_to_iid() returns. This hash directly derives the cryptographic IID - while the public key is public, predictable memory residue enables local attacks if an attacker can read process memory.

Fix: Use explicit_bzero() (POSIX), memset_s() (C11 Annex K), or Zephyr's secure_memzero() if available.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/ipv6_addr.c:87
close_reason: Replaced memset() with secure_zero() for hash buffer at line 100 in ipv6_addr.c. Added secure_zero helper with volatile pointer to prevent compiler optimization.
